key_items: Bitcoin price craters to $60,000 as BTC bulls get jobs report they were hoping to avoid -- May payrolls beat was hawkish enough to pressure crypto
Bitcoin Has Dumped All of Its Gains Since Trump Was Reelected—And Then Some -- Bitcoin down more than 50% from peak deep into 2025
Bitcoin bears face $2.6B trap as BTC funding rate drops: Is a short squeeze brewing? -- High leverage positions suggest potential reversal but current momentum is down
reasoning: Bitcoin is experiencing significant selling pressure with a 50% decline from 2025 peaks, driven by hawkish macroeconomic data (strong jobs report), institutional selling (BlackRock $213M dump, Strategy's disclosure), and technical breakdown below $60K support. While extreme funding rates and fear metrics (Fear Index at 12) suggest capitulation and potential reversal setup, the near-term momentum remains decidedly bearish with 48+ days of continuous selling pressure on major exchanges.
risks: Further macroeconomic hawkishness or break below $59K could trigger capitulation cascade and test $40K support levels before any meaningful bounce.
